question_groups.instruction: Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

items.prompt_html: There are a few surviving documents which show that a game similar to tennis was played in the ancient world.
An early form of tennis involved players hitting a ball with just their hand.
The game of table tennis developed around the same time as tennis.
Tennis became popular with all sections of French society in the 14th and 15th centuries.
The indoor courts created in the 14th and 15th centuries were larger than the outdoor courtyards.
There were attempts to prevent French aristocrats from playing tennis.

options.label: TRUE
options.text: if the statement agrees with the information

options.label: FALSE
options.text: if the statement contradicts the information

options.label: NOT GIVEN
options.text: if there is no information on this

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the summary below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Complete the summary below.

Write ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

A New Approach to Knowledge

John Ray was a scholar and self-taught botanist, whose work reflected the <blank data-qid="q21"/> that was taking place during the 17th century in people’s way of thinking about the natural world. This new approach is the basis of the modern field of <blank data-qid="q22"/>. It represented a complete break from the ideas of Aristotle, which had dominated thinking up until that time.

As Ray himself explained, his interest in plants was aroused after graduating, when he had to spend time outdoors after a period of <blank data-qid="q23"/>. He taught himself, in addition to many Cambridge <blank data-qid="q24"/>, and began compiling his catalogue of plants, which was published in 1660.

options.label: A
options.text: His teacher recommended that he should go to university.

options.label: B
options.text: His school record was not exceptional in any way.

options.label: C
options.text: He was helped by a former student of Cambridge University.

options.label: D
options.text: His parents were both valued by the people in their local area.

options.label: E
options.text: He originally planned to take up his father’s occupation.
